focus: President's Day Holiday • RBNZ/AUD CPI Week Ahead • China Stimulus Speculation
marketOverview: Markets enter holiday-thinned week with focus shifting to Wednesday's twin catalysts: RBNZ meeting and Australian CPI • China stimulus speculation builds as Xi Jinping chairs Politburo meeting • US/Canada markets closed for President's Day/Family Day • EUR consolidates below as ECB pushes back on rate cut expectations • JPY holds gains near as BoJ March hike pricing firms at 60%
marketFocus: Markets enter holiday-thinned week with focus shifting to Wednesday's twin catalysts: RBNZ meeting and Australian CPI
China stimulus speculation builds as Xi Jinping chairs Politburo meeting
US/Canada markets closed for President's Day/Family Day
EUR consolidates below as ECB pushes back on rate cut expectations
JPY holds gains near as BoJ March hike pricing firms at 60%

USD.summary: Dollar range-bound ahead of mid-week data deluge. Markets closed for President's Day. Volatile positioning at record bearish levels in Bank of America survey. Focus shifts to Tuesday's Empire State Manufacturing, Wednesday: FOMC Meeting Minutes
drivers: Dollar range-bound ahead of mid-week data deluge
Volatile positioning at record bearish levels in Bank of America survey

EUR.summary: EUR consolidates below as ECB pushes back on dovish repricing. Lagarde's comments and repo backstop expansion generated no meaningful FX reaction. Industrial Production data mixed. Range-bound until fresh catalysts emerge.
drivers: EUR consolidates below as ECB pushes back on dovish repricing
Lagarde's comments generated no FX reaction
Friday: German Flash Manufacturing PMI, Flash Services PMI

GBP.summary: GBP remains under pressure after dismal GDP weighs on sentiment. Manufacturing Production fell. Tuesday: UK employment data showed Claimant Count Change, Average Earnings fell, Unemployment Rate. Wednesday: CPI - key catalyst for BoE timing. Political uncertainty persists despite Cabinet support for Reeves. GBP/USD testing support, vulnerable to further downside on weak data.
drivers: Tuesday: Claimant Count Change, Average Earnings fell, Unemployment Rate
Wednesday: CPI - key catalyst for BoE timing
GBP/USD testing support, vulnerable to further downside

JPY.summary: JPY holds post-Takaichi rally gains near as BoJ March hike pricing firms at 60%. USD/JPY sharp reversal sparked intervention speculation. Monday: Tertiary Industry Activity. Tuesday: Trade Balance. Carry trade unwind continues as global yields compress. JPY benefits from risk-off positioning ahead of RBNZ/AUD CPI Wednesday.
drivers: BoJ March hike odds at 60% after Takaichi landslide election win
USD/JPY intervention speculation after sharp reversal
Carry trade unwind accelerates on compressed global yield differentials

AUD.summary: AUD reclaims on hawkish RBA rhetoric on inflation persistence. RBA Minutes confirmed Board's concern that inflation would stay stubbornly high without February hike. Wednesday: AUD CPI - key catalyst for RBA policy. Monday: RBA Meeting Minutes. Thursday: Wage Price Index. China stimulus speculation provides additional tailwind. AUD/USD testing resistance, breakout risk on strong CPI.
drivers: Hawkish RBA rhetoric on inflation persistence
Wednesday: AUD CPI - key catalyst for RBA policy

CAD.summary: CAD under sustained pressure from Trump considering exiting USMCA. New Chief Trade Negotiator appointed to manage US negotiations. Markets closed for Family Day holiday. Tuesday: CPI - key catalyst for BoC policy. Oil prices steady but geopolitical risks loom. CAD vulnerable to further upside on weak CPI print.
drivers: Markets closed for Family Day holiday
Tuesday: CPI - key catalyst for BoC policy
Oil prices steady but geopolitical risks loom

CHF.summary: CHF consolidates after AI disruption site-wide pause. Carry trade disadvantage limits upside despite low rates. SNB's ultra-low rates make CHF less quality flows. EUR/CHF stable near, lacks directional conviction. Safe-haven demand muted despite geopolitical tensions.
drivers: CHF consolidates after AI disruption site-wide pause
SNB's ultra-low rates make CHF less attractive

NZD.summary: NZD strengthens on high-beta risk appetite and RBNZ meeting anticipation. Markets expect RBNZ to hold rates at 60% after Breman's hawkish signals extend pause. Wednesday: RBNZ meeting, Inflation expectations remain firm. Official Cash Rate, Press Conference. Friday: RBNZ Gov Breman Speaks. NZD/USD testing resistance, breakout risk on hawkish RBNZ.
drivers: Wednesday: Official Cash Rate, Press Conference

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: Yields soften as markets price in Fed pause amid record bearish USD positioning. Fed speakers (Barr, Daly) reinforce data-dependent stance. President's Day holiday thins liquidity.

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bunds range-bound as ECB pushes back on aggressive dovish repricing. Lagarde's comments generate no meaningful reaction. German ZEW data ahead could provide direction.

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: JGB yields fall as weak GDP data contrasts with BoJ March hike odds at 60%. Takaichi landslide win reinforces policy normalization path. Carry trade unwind supports bonds.

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts steady ahead of key UK employment and CPI data this week. BoE April cut pricing remains on the table after 5-4 split in February. Political uncertainty persists.
